items.title: People in urgent need to jump social housing waitlist queue in WA

children.content: The WA government announces plans to undertake the first reform of the social housing waitlist in more than 70 years, with families fleeing domestic violence and homeless people to be among those prioritised for housing.

gallery.altText: A close up of John Carey delivering a press conference

thumbnail.altText: A close up of John Carey delivering a press conference

items.title: Mother of murdered Broome man slams WA Police culture

children.content: Ingrid Bishop says poor culture within WA Police hampered the investigation into her son Josh Warneke's death from the start.

image.altText: A woman in a floral shirt speaks to reporters outside court

items.title: Tree advocates dismayed as Melville council backtracks on canopy protection

children.content: Plans to protect established trees on private property have had a setback in Perth's southern suburbs, with councillors concerned that protections may go too far.

image.altText: Two skyrise buildings with green trees in the foreground.

items.title: 'Extraordinary waste': Accused criminals flown from remote areas as courts close

children.content: WA Police have been forced to fly some accused criminals out of remote communities since the start of this month due to regional court closures sparked by a security dispute.

gallery.altText: a small brick courthouse with two benches outside

thumbnail.altText: a small brick courthouse with two benches outside

items.title: Tim Wilson holds undisclosed shares in climate advisory firm through family trust

children.content: The shadow treasurer's investment trust holds 15,000 shares in a small company called Gondwana Carbon, which helps developers to acquire land, develop carbon abatement projects, earn carbon credits and then trade them.

image.altText: Tim Wilson at National Press Club

items.title: Government to extend pay subsidy to avoid childcare worker walk off

children.content: Early educators had threatened to walk off the job to keep a federally funded 15 per cent pay subsidy due to end in November.

image.altText: Unidentified child's hands playing with building bricks.

images.altText: Nicolas Jackson holds his hands to his face

children.content: Day six at the World Cup features a number of superstar strikers, beginning with a blockbuster between Kylian Mbappe's France and Senegal at New York New Jersey Stadium. Follow live.

items.title: Mbappe scores stunner after Aussie ref's 'nonsensical' VAR call
Joyce cautions Hanson ahead of National Press Club address

children.content: Barnaby Joyce has cautioned his leader Pauline Hanson against falling for "gotcha questions" during her address to the National Press Club today. Follow live.

image.altText: Barnaby Joyce gestures while speaking at press conference.

items.title: 'I didn't have a life': Pensioner moves to Thailand for cheaper living

children.content: Peter Stevenson says moving to Thailand has allowed him to live more comfortably on his Australian Age Pension after struggling to cover rent and essentials in regional Victoria.

image.altText: A selfie of a smiling man in silver-rimmed glasses and a grey tee taken from inside a restauraunt with red lanterns.

items.title: El Niño declared by BOM and it could become the strongest on record

children.content: El Niño is a state that can linger for up to 12 months and disrupt weather patterns across the globe, with Australia especially prone.

image.altText: A graphic of the Earth with the majority of areas highlighted in yellow and orange indicating warmer weather around Aus.

items.title: Gout runs a stunning third as Lyles breaks world record

children.content: Rising Australian superstar Gout Gout pushes American champion Noah Lyles all the way in their first race against each other.

image.altText: Two men hug after a running race.

items.title: 'It's all too hard': Fuel shock pushes regional businesses to the brink

children.content: Small-town coffee shops and long-established businesses are at risk of closing despite a ceasefire in the Middle East easing the global oil price. Regional businesses say fuel relief measures need to be extended because the damage done from the fuel shock is far from over.

items.title: Dealing with NDIS leading to anxiety and suicidal ideation among participants

children.content: Participants say they are "exhausted" and "overwhelmed" by trying to navigate the complex and ever-changing NDIS system.

image.altText: Andrew Bretherton dressed in pants and dark jumper and jacket sits in a wheelchair.

items.title: Looking for something to play? Here are the best games of 2026 so far

children.content: From cosy builders to hectic shooters and all the way through to nostalgic adventures, there are lots of gems to enjoy on your favourite game console so far this year.

image.altText: Three people shaped characters stand with a pokemon trubbish

items.title: Wharfie Time or crunch time, these are the AFL teams coming up clutch

children.content: The Dockers use Wharfie Time to get a big boost from their fans, while everyone else toils away in what they call crunch time. In an AFL season full of tight finishes, which teams are holding their nerve best and why?

items.title: Ann Patchett on why stalkers want her to read their novels

children.content: The bestselling author of books including Bel Canto, Tom Lake and her latest, Whistler, is determined to see the good in everyone.

image.altText: Ann Patchett, a woman with short greying hair, wearing a floral dress, sitting in front a series of bookshelves.

items.title: Ben Roberts-Smith's bail conditions varied to attend war memorial event

children.content: War veteran Ben Roberts-Smith's bail conditions have been varied so he can attend an opening at the Australian War Memorial in Canberra next week.

image.altText: Ben Roberts-Smith exiting Downing Centre Sydney, smiling, in a navy suit.

items.title: Mother of Perth girl shot dead in Pakistan 'in extreme shock'

children.content: A nine-year-old girl fatally shot by police who were pursuing armed robbers in Pakistan is remembered as happy and bubbly by the principal of her Perth school, as her family tries to deal with their "tragic loss".

articles.title: Police officer in custody after Perth girl shot dead overseas

children.content: A nine-year-old Perth girl has been fatally shot by police in Pakistan after officers mistook her family's car for that of suspected criminals.

image.altText: A silhouette of two Pakistani police officers holding rifles at a barrier or gate of some sort.

items.title: Woman denied bail over alleged plot to smuggle fugitive bikie out of WA

children.content: Queensland woman Jessica Beckett told a West Australian court she did not know an outlaw bikie gang member was on the boat she was sailing to Indonesia.

items.title: 'Worrying' surge in people injecting peptides in WA

children.content: Needle exchanges in Western Australia are reporting dramatic increases in people accessing their services for peptide use.

image.altText: A collection of small clear vials containing different coloured liquid.

items.title: Picturesque islands in WA's north set to become transport hubs after $20m sale

children.content: A Kimberley iron ore mine with idyllic views across a tropical sea will close after 70 years of operation as a new phase begins for offshore oil and gas.
